paper.title_en: Reducing the short-range uncertainty in the nuclear matrix elements for neutrinoless double-<math><mi>β</mi></math> decay
paper.abstract_en: The nuclear matrix elements (NMEs) of the neutrinoless double-<math><mi>β</mi></math> (<math><mrow><mn>0</mn><mi>ν</mi><mi>β</mi><mi>β</mi></mrow></math>) decay in the candidate nuclei relevant to the current and next-generation experimental searches are crucial for extracting beyond-standard-model parameters from the experimental half-life measurements. However, the recently recognized short-range operator with an unknown coupling, required by the renormalizability of the leading-order <math><mrow><mi>n</mi><mi>n</mi><mo>→</mo><mi>p</mi><mi>p</mi><mi>e</mi><mi>e</mi></mrow></math> amplitudes within the framework of chiral effective field theory (EFT), has introduced significant uncertainties into the NMEs. In this Letter, the <math><mrow><mn>0</mn><mi>ν</mi><mi>β</mi><mi>β</mi></mrow></math>-decay NMEs of <math><mmultiscripts><mi>Ca</mi><mprescripts/><none/><mn>48</mn></mmultiscripts><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mmultiscripts><mi>Ge</mi><mprescripts/><none/><mn>76</mn></mmultiscripts><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mmultiscripts><mi>Se</mi><mprescripts/><none/><mn>82</mn></mmultiscripts><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mmultiscripts><mi>Mo</mi><mprescripts/><none/><mn>100</mn></mmultiscripts></math>, and <math><mmultiscripts><mi>Xe</mi><mprescripts/><none/><mn>136</mn></mmultiscripts></math> are calculated using, for the first time, the decay operator derived from the relativistic chiral EFT and the relativistic many-body wave functions obtained from the state-of-the-art relativistic configuration-interaction density functional theory. The resulting NMEs are free from the uncertainties typically introduced by leading-order short-range operators, as the relativistic chiral decay operator naturally ensures the renormalizability of the <math><mrow><mi>n</mi><mi>n</mi><mo>→</mo><mi>p</mi><mi>p</mi><mi>e</mi><mi>e</mi></mrow></math> amplitudes at leading order, obviating the need for a short-range term. The present NMEs are then compared with those obtained using the nonrelativistic chiral decay operators, which includes both long- and short-range terms, and the short-range uncertainties are analyzed comprehensively. This study significantly reduces a key source of uncertainty in the evaluation of <math><mrow><mn>0</mn><mi>ν</mi><mi>β</mi><mi>β</mi></mrow></math>-decay NMEs. The reported NMEs without contamination of the leading-order short-range uncertainties are important for assessing the sensitivity to the inverted neutrino-mass hierarchy in next-generation experiments.

reasons: 未达到主题关键词门槛
